name: machine-spi
description: SPI (Serial Peripheral Interface) bus communication. Both software (SoftSPI) and hardware (SPI) available. ESP32 has 2 hardware SPI peripherals (HSPI=1, VSPI=2).
differences_from_cpython: Does NOT exist in CPython. MicroPython-specific SPI interface.

classes.name: SoftSPI
classes.signature: machine.SoftSPI(baudrate=1000000, *, polarity=0, phase=0, bits=8, sck, mosi, miso)
classes.description: Software (bit-banged) SPI. Works on any GPIO pins.

constructor_params.name: baudrate
constructor_params.description: SCK clock rate in Hz. Default 1_000_000 (1MHz).

constructor_params.name: phase
constructor_params.description: SPI phase (CPHA): 0=sample first edge, 1=sample second edge.

constructor_params.name: mosi
constructor_params.description: MOSI pin (Master Out Slave In). Required.

constructor_params.name: miso
constructor_params.description: MISO pin (Master In Slave Out). Required.

methods.name: SoftSPI.init
methods.signature: init(baudrate=1000000, *, polarity=0, phase=0, bits=8, firstbit=MSB, sck=None, mosi=None, miso=None)
methods.description: Initialize SPI peripheral with given parameters.

methods.name: SoftSPI.read
methods.description: Read nbytes while writing the given value.

methods.name: SoftSPI.readinto
methods.description: Read into buffer while writing the given value.

methods.name: SoftSPI.write_readinto
methods.description: Write from write_buf while reading into read_buf simultaneously.

classes.name: SPI
classes.signature: machine.SPI(id, baudrate=1000000, *, polarity=0, phase=0, bits=8, sck=None, mosi=None, miso=None)
classes.description: Hardware SPI. ESP32: id=1 (HSPI), id=2 (VSPI). id=2 is the default VSPI on most dev boards.

constructor_params.name: baudrate
constructor_params.description: Clock rate in Hz. Default 1MHz. Max ~80MHz (half APB clock).

constructor_params.name: sck
constructor_params.description: Clock pin. None = use default pin for this id.

methods.name: SPI.init
methods.signature: init(baudrate=1000000, *, polarity=0, phase=0, bits=8, firstbit=MSB, sck=None, mosi=None, miso=None)
methods.description: Initialize SPI peripheral with given parameters.

methods.name: SPI.read
methods.description: Read nbytes while writing the given value.

methods.name: SPI.readinto
methods.description: Read into buffer while writing the given value.

methods.name: SPI.write_readinto
methods.description: Write from write_buf while reading into read_buf simultaneously.

shared_methods.name: SPI.init
shared_methods.signature: init(baudrate=1000000, *, polarity=0, phase=0, bits=8, sck=None, mosi=None, miso=None)

shared_methods.name: SPI.deinit
shared_methods.description: Turn off SPI and release pins.

shared_methods.name: SPI.read
shared_methods.description: Read nbytes while writing constant value (default 0x00).

shared_methods.name: SPI.readinto
shared_methods.description: Read into buffer while writing constant value.

shared_methods.name: SPI.write_readinto
shared_methods.description: Write from write_buf and simultaneously read into read_buf.

example:
from machine import Pin, SPI, SoftSPI

# Hardware SPI (VSPI, default pins)
spi = SPI(2, baudrate=10000000, polarity=0, phase=0)

# Software SPI (any pins)
# spi = SoftSPI(baudrate=5000000, sck=Pin(18), mosi=Pin(23), miso=Pin(19))

# Write to device, then read response
spi.write(b'\xAA\xBB')   # send command
data = spi.read(4)        # read 4 bytes response

# Full-duplex transfer
write_buf = b'\x01\x02\x03'
read_buf = bytearray(3)
spi.write_readinto(write_buf, read_buf)
print('Received:', read_buf)
esp32_notes: ESP32 HSPI default: sck=14, mosi=13, miso=12 (id=1).
ESP32 VSPI default: sck=18, mosi=23, miso=19 (id=2). Used by SDCard slot=2.
Hardware SPI can use DMA for fast transfers; SoftSPI cannot.
Max hardware SPI baudrate: 80MHz (limited by APB clock; practical max ~40MHz).
For SD card, use machine.SDCard which wraps SPI internally.
